data.reasoning: NVIDIA Blackwell multi-year deal signals sustained data-center demand with concrete incremental revenue beginning next quarter. However, this represents a material concentration of upside optionality in a single product cycle transition carrying execution, competitive, and macro headwinds. Key risks requiring committee oversight: (1) Blackwell ramp execution — architectural transition at scale with binary adoption risk from hyperscaler customer base; (2) Competitive intensity — AMD's EPYC GPU portfolio and hyperscaler custom silicon (TPU, Trainium, Gaudi) increasingly viable alternatives, particularly if Blackwell delays occur; (3) Macro/capex cycle — cloud infrastructure spending is cyclical and sensitive to AI ROI realization; top-3 customer concentration amplifies impact of any demand deceleration; (4) Geopolitical — U.S.-China export restrictions could materially shrink addressable market if enforcement tightens or customer base shifts. Position size (~$75.5k) is material but the multi-year contract de-risks near-term visibility. Committee judgment needed on whether Blackwell execution confidence and competitive moat assumptions warrant concentration in a single mega-cap growth narrative.
